name: Continuous Drive-Gradient Reactive Policy (Algebraic/Geometric)
description: A memoryless, reactive policy that selects actions by computing the immediate expected drive reduction for each candidate action using a gradient-descent heuristic over the drive landscape. No Q-table or learned values are maintained — the agent computes one-step-ahead utility for each action by combining (a) the immediate drive change from eating, (b) the proximity gain toward the nearest food source weighted by current drive level (implementing alliesthesia from Cabanac 1971), and (c) the metabolic cost of staying still. Based on Keramati & Gutkin (2014) postulates P1 and P4, and Hull's (1943) drive-reduction theory. This yields a fast, interpretable agent that explicitly demonstrates state-dependent reward valuation without requiring learning.

variables.name: hunger_level
variables.description: Current hunger deficit level. 0 = fully sated, higher = hungrier. Increases by metabolic drift each step, decreases when food is consumed.

variables.name: homeostatic_setpoint
variables.description: Ideal internal state (no deficit). Fixed constant.

variables.name: drive
variables.description: Scalar deviation cost: D(h_t) = m * |h_t - h*|^n. Monotonically increasing with deficit.

variables.name: resource_positions
variables.description: Set of (x, y) positions currently containing food resources, read from perception.

variables.name: nearest_food_distance
variables.description: Manhattan distance from a position to the nearest food resource.

variables.name: action_utility
variables.description: Computed utility for each candidate action, combining drive reduction and proximity gain.

variables.name: eat_drive_reduction
variables.description: Immediate drive reduction from eating at current position: D(h_t) - D(clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K, 0, h_max)).

variables.name: proximity_gain
variables.description: Change in distance to nearest food from taking movement action a: d_nearest(s_t) - d_nearest(s_t + delta(a)). Positive = getting closer.

variables.name: ate_food_flag
variables.description: Boolean (0 or 1) indicating successful food consumption this step.

parameters.name: proximity_weight
parameters.source: Tuned: relative weight of approach behavior vs. immediate drive reduction

parameters.name: satiation_threshold
parameters.source: Drive level below which eating is suppressed; prevents over-consumption (Cabanac 1971 alliesthesia)

rules.description: Drive function — quadratic deviation from setpoint
Immediate drive reduction from eating (one-step lookahead with metabolic drift included)
rules.pseudocode:
h_after_eat = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K, 0, h_max)
delta_D_eat = D(h_t) - D(h_after_eat)
# where D(x) = m * abs(x - h_star) ** n

rules.description: Proximity gain for movement action — reduction in Manhattan distance to nearest food
rules.pseudocode:
# Compute position after action
delta_map = {'move_up': (0,-1), 'move_down': (0,1), 'move_left': (-1,0), 'move_right': (1,0)}
new_pos = (clip(s_t[0] + dx, 0, grid_w-1), clip(s_t[1] + dy, 0, grid_h-1))

# d_nearest(pos) = min Manhattan distance from pos to any food in R
# If R is empty, d_nearest = grid_width + grid_height (large fallback)
d_current = d_nearest(s_t)
d_after = d_nearest(new_pos)
Prox_a = d_current - d_after

rules.description: Drive-weighted proximity utility for movement (alliesthesia modulation)
rules.pseudocode:
U_move_a = w_prox * D_t * Prox_a
# When D_t ≈ 0 (sated), U_move ≈ 0 regardless of proximity → alliesthesia

rules.description: Utility of eating — gated by satiation threshold
Utility of staying — always incurs metabolic drift cost
rules.pseudocode:
D_after_stay = m * abs(h_t + lambda_drift - h_star) ** n
U_stay = -(D_after_stay - D_t)
# Always <= 0: staying only costs drive

rules.description: Softmax action selection over all utilities
rules.pseudocode:
utilities = [U_move_up, U_move_down, U_move_left, U_move_right, U_stay, U_eat]
max_u = max(utilities)
exp_vals = [exp(beta * (u - max_u)) for u in utilities]
sum_exp = sum(exp_vals)
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
a_t = random_choice(all_actions, probs)

rules.description: Internal state update after action (metabolic drift + consumption)
rules.pseudocode: h_t = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)

decision_logic.description: Reactive (memoryless) policy: the agent perceives its position, hunger, and food locations, then computes a one-step utility for every action. Eating utility is the immediate drive reduction, gated by a satiation threshold to prevent over-consumption. Movement utility is the proximity gain toward the nearest food, multiplied by current drive (so a sated agent is indifferent — alliesthesia). Staying incurs metabolic drift cost. Action is sampled via softmax over utilities. No Q-table or learning is involved; after acting, only the internal hunger state is updated.
pseudocode: R = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list]
food_at_position = (s_t[0], s_t[1]) in [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list]
if not R: return grid_w + grid_h
return min(abs(pos[0]-r[0]) + abs(pos[1]-r[1]) for r in R)
D_after_stay = m * abs(h_t + lambda_drift - h_star) ** n
exp_vals = [exp(beta * (u - max_u)) for u in u_vals]
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
ate_t = 1 if (action == 'eat' and new_perception.get('last_action_result', {}).get('consumed', False)) else 0
h_t = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)
# No Q-table update — reactive policy. Optionally refresh R from new_perception.

perception_to_variables.food_at_position: any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources']['food'])

env_mapping.reward_source: Internally computed as drive reduction utilities. Environment reward from ConsumeEffect (reward=1.0) is NOT used — the agent relies on its own homeostatic utility computation.

expected_behaviors.description: When hungry (high drive), agent moves toward nearest food
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 8.0, place food 5 cells away → run 5 steps → assert Manhattan distance to food decreases

expected_behaviors.description: When sated (low drive), agent is indifferent to food location (alliesthesia)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 0.0 → compute utilities for movement actions → assert all U_move ≈ 0 regardless of food proximity

expected_behaviors.description: Agent eats when on food cell and hungry, but refuses to eat when sated (satiation gate)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 5.0 on food cell → assert eat selected with high probability; set h_t = 0.2 (< eta=0.5) → assert eat NOT selected

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger drifts upward without food consumption
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps without eating → assert h_t = min(50 * lambda_drift, h_max)

expected_behaviors.description: Drive-proportional approach speed: higher hunger leads to more directed movement toward food
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: compare P(move_toward_food) at h_t=2 vs h_t=8 → assert P is higher at h_t=8

references: Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2014). Homeostatic reinforcement learning for integrating reward collection and physiological stability. eLife 3:e04811.
Cabanac, M. (1971). Physiological role of pleasure. Science.
